title: subagent: BackgroundAgentManager silently swallows Mutex poisoning, masking corruption
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/subagent.rs:294-296 (register), src/subagent.rs:302-304 (get), src/subagent.rs:307-313 (finish), src/subagent.rs:317-323 (fail), src/subagent.rs:333-349 (list)
## Violation
Every BackgroundAgentManager method uses `self.agents.lock().ok()` / `if let Ok(mut a) = self.agents.lock()`, silently treating a `PoisonError` as a successful no-op:

```rust
pub fn register(&self, agent_type: AgentType, task: &str) -> String {
    ...
    if let Ok(mut agents) = self.agents.lock() {
        agents.insert(id.clone(), agent);
    }
    id  // returned even if insert never happened
}
```

If any code path panics while holding the lock (e.g., during `register`/`finish`), every subsequent call appears to succeed but operates on no data. `register` returns an id that was never inserted. `agent_output` then reports the agent "not found" forever — with no error trail showing the poisoning event.

Same pattern in `result.lock().ok()` for the BackgroundAgent inner mutexes (lines 308-310, 318-321, 423-424).

## Mandated refactor
Either (a) propagate `PoisonError` as a typed `RegistryError::Poisoned` so callers can recover or abort, or (b) call `.lock().unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner)` to recover the inner data and log a `tracing::error!` event. Silent no-op is the worst of all options.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
